description_long: This is a large, double-edged battle axe with a broad, jagged steel blade featuring two prominent cutouts and a pointed tip. The blade is attached to a long, slightly curved wooden handle reinforced with metal bands and decorative elements. The handle appears to be ergonomically shaped, with a thicker grip section and metal end cap for balance and durability. The blade has a rough, hammered texture, giving it a rugged, medieval appearance. The overall construction suggests it is designed for heavy-duty use, likely as a weapon or ceremonial tool. The color palette consists mainly of metallic gray for the blade and warm brown for the wooden handle, accented by silver metal fittings.
description: A large, jagged steel-bladed battle axe with a curved wooden handle reinforced by metal bands and decorative elements. The blade features two cutouts and a pointed tip, giving it a rugged, medieval look.
description_view_0: The axe is shown at an angle, highlighting the broad, jagged blade with two cutouts and the curved wooden handle with metal reinforcements.
description_view_1: A direct side view showing the thin profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle, emphasizing the pointed tip.
description_view_2: The opposite angle of the axe, again displaying the jagged blade with cutouts and the full length of the handle.
description_view_3: A direct front view showing the full width of the blade, the two large cutouts, and the symmetrical design of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic grip, reinforced by metal bands, oval cross-section, and a metal end cap.
attribute_blade_design: Large, jagged steel blade with two prominent cutouts, pointed tip, double-edged, and rough hammered texture.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible metal reinforcements securing the blade to the handle.
